brand.name: Mile Hi City Pickleball
brand.intro: Nine voices. One court. Every week, fresh stories from the players shaping pickleball culture in Colorado.

thisWeek.title: pov: first pickle ever
thisWeek.brief: Single creator on Court 3 holding a JOOLA paddle backwards, looking lost. Hook (0-3s): big text 'pov: your friends dragged you to Mile Hi and you've literally never held a paddle.' Cut to chaotic rally — whiffs, laughs, one accidental winner, Coach Jill walking by giving a thumbs up. Payoff: end on the navy 'GAME ON' wall with text 'okay i get it now.' Shaky-cam, friend-filmed, zero polish. The whole point is the 'i swore i'd hate this' to 'okay one more game' arc beginners live.
thisWeek.trendSource: Beginner-POV pickleball is an evergreen-but-spiking format on TikTok — @thedinkpickleball's 'first time' POV pulled big numbers and the format is still being remixed weekly in 2026. Gen Z is now 28.8% of pickleball players (Woke Waves), the sport's largest demo.

thisWeek.title: rock music paddle reveal
thisWeek.brief: Use Charli XCX's 'Rock Music' (dropped May 7, currently TikTok's #1 edit sound). Hook (0-3s): one of the girls walks onto the 'GAME ON' navy court in plain clothes, paddle behind her back. Sync to the vocal glitch mid-song and use IG's 'stuck frame' to freeze her mid-spin — she lands in full tennis-skirt fit with the JOOLA paddle. Payoff: rally starts, squad joins in on each beat drop. Looks like a hard edit, isn't. The audio does the work — just nail the timing on the glitch.
thisWeek.trendSource: Charli XCX 'Rock Music' glitch edit is one of the biggest formats on TikTok right now (NewEngen June 2026 trend report). The deliberate audio glitch + Instagram 'stuck frame' animation = transition that 'looks harder than it is.' Perfect for a fit + court reveal.

thisWeek.title: wow, ok — pickleball edition
thisWeek.brief: Adapt the viral 'wow, ok' acting-range challenge. Two creators face the camera courtside (navy wall behind them) and deliver the same line — 'wow, ok' — four ways after each scenario: 1) supportive (partner hits a winner), 2) disappointed (partner whiffs an easy dink), 3) sarcastic (opponent claims a ball was in), 4) flirty (cute stranger asks to join their game). On-screen text numbers all four so the comments rank them. No music, just original audio. Bad acting IS the joke — overcommit.
thisWeek.trendSource: The 'wow, ok' acting-range challenge is the breakout no-audio format on the FYP in June 2026 (NewEngen + TikTok Discover). Lowest-lift performance trend going — viewers debate the reads in the comments, which is engagement gold for a brand account.

thisWeek.title: grwm to play mile hi
thisWeek.brief: Tenniscore GRWM ('get ready with me') adapted for indoor pickleball. Start in the locker area: pleated skirt, white socks pulled up, hair ribbon, sunscreen even though we're indoors (the joke). Voiceover walks through the fit. Cut to walking onto Court 1, paddle in hand, hitting the navy 'GAME ON' wall as the reveal. Payoff: first rally with the squad, on-screen text 'pickleball is just tennis for people who want to have fun.' Lean into the warm 70s retro vibe — slight grain filter if possible.
thisWeek.trendSource: #Tenniscore has 92.5M+ posts on TikTok (Heuritech, BiggerThanSports) and GRWM-style racquet-sport content is a proven Gen Z format. Mile Hi's retro warm aesthetic maps perfectly onto the tenniscore moodboard.
